answer:
The equilibrium constant expression is the ratio of the concentrations of products raised to their stoichiometric coefficients divided by the concentrations of reactants raised to their coefficients. For the reaction \(4 \text{NH}_3(g) + 5 \text{O}_2(g) \rightleftarrows 4 \text{NO}(g) + 6 \text{H}_2\text{O}(g)\), this gives \(K = \frac{[\text{NO}]^4 [\text{H}_2\text{O}]^6}{[\text{NH}_3]^4 [\text{O}_2]^5}\).  

\boxed{C}
